text:
TAUCETI-VERDICT-b49faf6a12d9ad2d597e7316
{"verdict":"request_changes","summary":"The new theorem is a named one-off numeric fact rather than reusable API; it should not be added as a parallel declaration for an instance of existing positivity lemmas/tactics.","findings":[{"file":"TauCeti/Basic.lean","line":"16","issue":"`TauCeti.goliveCheck : 0 < 31` is a special-case named theorem for a concrete numeral, duplicating the role of existing general positivity facts/procedures rather than adding reusable library material.","fix":"Remove `goliveCheck`; where this fact is needed locally, use `norm_num`, `decide`, or a general positivity lemma inline instead of adding a named declaration.","evidence":"Diff adds `theorem goliveCheck : 0 < 31 := by norm_num`; the proof itself shows the result is discharged by the existing numeric API rather than requiring a new TauCeti declaration."}]}

verdict_obj.summary: The new theorem is a named one-off numeric fact rather than reusable API; it should not be added as a parallel declaration for an instance of existing positivity lemmas/tactics.

findings.issue: `TauCeti.goliveCheck : 0 < 31` is a special-case named theorem for a concrete numeral, duplicating the role of existing general positivity facts/procedures rather than adding reusable library material.
findings.fix: Remove `goliveCheck`; where this fact is needed locally, use `norm_num`, `decide`, or a general positivity lemma inline instead of adding a named declaration.
findings.evidence: Diff adds `theorem goliveCheck : 0 < 31 := by norm_num`; the proof itself shows the result is discharged by the existing numeric API rather than requiring a new TauCeti declaration.
